review_title: High-Quality Vitamin C with No Stomach Issues
review_text: I’ve been using California Gold Nutrition’s Gold C for a few weeks, and it’s been a great addition to my daily routine. The 1,000 mg dose of USP-grade vitamin C is exactly what I need for immune support, and I appreciate that the veggie capsules are gentle on my stomach. I’ve tried other high-dose vitamin C supplements in the past, but I’ve never had any issues with digestive discomfort or acid reflux with this one. I’ve been feeling more energetic, especially during seasonal transitions, and the 60-capsule bottle is a solid value for the price. Definitely recommend if you’re looking for a reliable and well-dosed vitamin C supplement.

I’ve already tried and reviewed other Vitamin C products, and compared to those, this one is just fair. The quality is not bad, but it doesn’t feel as pure or as well-made as brands like NOW Foods, which keep their formulations cleaner and more reliable. The ingredients are straightforward, and the dosage at 1000 mg per capsule is standard, but there isn’t anything that makes this product stand out. The size of the bottle (60 veggie capsules) also makes it a short supply if you’re taking it daily, which reduces its overall value. Overall, it does the job as a Vitamin C supplement, but there are definitely better options on the market if you want both quality and value.

Just keep in mind that when taking a supplement you need to consider all side effects and drug interactions first. review_title: Disappointing Purchase with Limited Shelf Life and Mediocre Features
review_text: The major drawback is the surprisingly short shelf life – only two months! It's frustrating to realize that I have to consume all these capsules within such a brief timeframe. The quality of the product is average, and while it boasts USP Grade Vitamin C, the ingredient list doesn't stand out. The size of the veggie capsules is reasonable, but the limited quantity due to the short shelf life is a letdown. The 1,000 mg dosage is decent for Vitamin C, but the overall effectiveness seems mediocre. Despite Vitamin C being renowned for its immunity-boosting properties, this supplement didn't deliver the expected results. The packaging is standard, without any remarkable features. The taste is fine, but that's not the primary concern with such supplements. Addressing Vitamin C deficiency is essential, and the short shelf life is a significant drawback. Absorption and overall health benefits are crucial, and this product doesn't seem to excel in these areas. I had higher expectations, and this purchase has left me unsatisfied. I won't be repurchasing due to the inconvenience of the expiration date and the lackluster features of the product. Buyers beware – check those expiration dates and product details before making a decision!

Hoping for immune reinforcement and overall well-being, my experience with the Vitamin C supplement was significantly underwhelming, leading me to assign it a lackluster 1-star rating. Despite its reputation for boosting immunity and promoting health, this supplement's inability to deliver noticeable benefits left me questioning its effectiveness and value. Anticipating heightened immune support and improved overall vitality, I was dismayed to find that the Vitamin C supplement didn't fulfill these aspirations. Despite consistent use, I didn't notice any substantial improvements in my immune system or overall well-being. The lack of results in these areas was a significant letdown. While Vitamin C is renowned for its role in immune function and overall health, the supplement fell short in translating these potential benefits into noticeable results. The absence of any significant effects on my immune system or overall vitality left me skeptical about its overall effectiveness. Furthermore, the taste and aftertaste of the supplement were far from enjoyable. The discomfort of consumption was compounded by the lack of tangible benefits, making the overall experience less than satisfactory. In conclusion, the Vitamin C supplement failed to provide the expected immune support and overall health benefits, despite its acclaim in these areas. The lack of substantial benefits and the unappetizing taste make this supplement a regrettable choice. I strongly advise exploring alternative immune support and well-being supplement options that offer more significant results and a more palatable experience.

I decided to try California Gold Nutrition, Gold C because of the good reviews and the brand's reputation. Everything sounds great in the description: pure USP-grade ascorbic acid, high dosage of 1000 mg, vegetable capsules, no gluten, soy or GMO. Overall, the composition is really decent, and my expectations were appropriate. Why I gave it two stars: 1. Side effects. Literally from the first dose, I began to feel discomfort in my stomach, especially if I took the capsule in the morning on an empty stomach. There was a feeling of irritation, sometimes slight nausea and bloating. I tried drinking it during meals - it got a little better, but still unpleasant. My gastrointestinal tract is not the most sensitive, and vitamin C has never caused such problems before. Apparently, the form of ascorbic acid in this dosage (1000 mg) is too "harsh". 2. No noticeable effect. I took it for almost a month, hoping to improve my immunity and overall tone. But I didn't feel any noticeable changes: no more energy, no less colds. Perhaps there would have been an effect in case of a deficiency, but for maintenance - it didn't show any effect at all. What I liked: The capsules are small, easy to swallow. The packaging is high-quality, the lid has a protective cover. Doesn't contain anything extra - the composition is all honest. Conclusion: The product may be of good quality, but it is not for everyone. It didn't suit me because of side effects from the gastrointestinal tract and the lack of any noticeable effect. Perhaps it is worth trying the buffered form of vitamin C - it is gentler on the stomach. Rating: 2 out of 5. I will not buy it again.

Your body needs vitamin C (ascorbic acid) to form blood vessels, cartilage, muscle and collagen in bones. Vitamin C is also essential for your body's recovery process. Vitamin C is an antioxidant that protects your body's cells from the effects of free radicals. These are molecules that arise as your body digests and breaks down food or when the body is exposed to tobacco smoke, sunlight, X-rays, or other sources. Free radicals may play a role in heart disease, cancer, and other diseases. Vitamin C also helps your body absorb and store iron. Since your body does not secrete vitamin C; You need to get it from your diet. Vitamin C is found in citrus fruits, berries, potatoes, tomatoes, peppers, cabbage, Brussels sprouts, broccoli, and spinach. Vitamin C is also available in oral supplements, usually in the form of capsules and chewable tablets.
